MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Dale, via ZOOM, for Patricia Doeden, Petitioner.

Counsel reports that a death in the family has caused the delay in obtaining documents required to prepare an accounting but advises that he will attempt to gather as much as he can and file a petition. Counsel requests a three-month continuance.

The Status of Accounting is continued to Jun-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Nov-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg appeared by zoom for Patricia Doeden.

The Court stated that this matter is on calendar because of the difference between what's in the trust and the declaration that was filed regarding the goal of the Trustee for the trust, raised the question of whether Article 3, Sections 1 and 3 should be modified restricting the Trustee's authority to make discretionary distributions noting that it says "can or may", not "must", and raised the issue of the tension between the parents responsibility to provide support for Andrew without invading the trust. Ms. Steneberg stated that the intent is as stated in the declaration, that they can make modifications to Article 3, Sections 1 and 3, that there is concern about limiting the scope and that they are not sure what Andrew's needs will be. Ms. Steneberg also stated that she is happy to change language from "may" to "shall" with an amount not to exceed $1,000.00 for major expenditures and add a sentence addressing the obligation of parental support, and that she can have a revised order to the Court in two to three days. The Petition for Order Directing Establishment of Minor's Settlement Trust, etc. is continued to November 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Nov-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg, Esq., Counsel for Patricia Doeden, proposed Guardian ad Litem for Andrew Doeden, minor (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes at the outset that Ms. Doeden has not yet been appointed Guardian ad Litem for her son, Andrew. Ms. Steneberg reports that it was her understanding that Ms. Doeden was appointed Guardian ad Litem for the minor in the underlying civil litigation only to learn that Andrew's father was the Guardian ad Litem in that case. An ex parte Petition to appoint Ms. Doeden as Guardian ad Litem for the minor for purposes of the Settlement Trust is now pending.

The Court advises counsel that there are some additional concerns with the proposed trust otherwise highlighted in the Examiner Notes which will need to be addressed. Ms. Steneberg also notes that the Minor's Compromise is now pending in Dept. 514 and is trailing resolution of this matter.

The Court continues the matter to November 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)
